subject: Chemical Engineering Thermodynamics
name: Liquid/Liquid Equilibrium and Consolute Points
desc: Liquid/liquid equilibrium occurs when two immiscible or partially miscible liquids coexist. The binodal curve on a $T$‑$x$ diagram shows the compositions of the two phases. The upper consolute temperature (UCST) is the highest temperature at which two phases exist; the lower consolute temperature (LCST) is the lowest temperature. At consolute points, the two phases become identical.
trap: 🚨 Some systems exhibit only a UCST, some only an LCST, and some both. The existence of LLE depends on the temperature and the shape of the $\Delta G$ curve.

subject: Chemical Engineering Thermodynamics
name: Vapor/Liquid/Liquid Equilibrium (VLLE)
desc: When two liquid phases and one vapor phase coexist at equilibrium, the system has $F=1$ for a binary mixture. On a $Txy$ diagram at constant $P$, VLLE appears as a horizontal line (three‑phase line) connecting the two liquid compositions and the vapor composition. Above this line, two‑phase vapor‑liquid regions exist; below, liquid‑liquid equilibrium.
formula: $F = 1$ for a binary system with three phases.
trap: 🚨 At the three‑phase line, the temperature and compositions are fixed for a given pressure; adding more of either component changes only the phase amounts, not the phase compositions.
